assertion_results.text: Impact map or task descriptions explicitly flag at least 3 ambiguities from the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: The impact map (impact-map.md) explicitly flags 5 ambiguities: (1) 'No quantitative performance target for "faster" search', (2) 'No definition of "more relevant" results', (3) 'No specification for "Add filters"', (4) '"Better UI" excluded from scope', (5) 'Scope of "Don\'t break existing functionality"'. All five are clearly labeled as 'Ambiguity 1' through 'Ambiguity 5' in the Ambiguities Identified section.

assertion_results.text: Plan acknowledges that 'Better UI' (non-MVP) cannot be planned without design mockups or frontend repository and excludes it from scope
assertion_results.evidence: Impact map Ambiguity 4 states: '"Better UI" excluded from scope. The requirement "Better UI — Make it look nicer" is marked non-MVP. Furthermore, the target repository (trustify-backend) is a Rust backend service with no frontend code. No design mockups or Figma links are provided. No frontend repository is listed in the Repository Registry. Decision: This requirement is excluded from scope entirely. It cannot be planned without design mockups, a frontend repository, and clarification of what "nicer" means.' The Excluded from Scope section also reiterates: 'Better UI (non-MVP): Cannot be planned — no design mockups, no frontend repository in Registry, and requirement is non-MVP. Requires Figma designs and a frontend repository before planning.'

assertion_results.text: Tasks still follow the task-description-template.md format despite ambiguous requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All four tasks follow the template format. Each contains the required sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, Verification Commands, Dependencies. Optional sections are used appropriately: Files to Create in task 1 (the only task creating new files), API Changes in task 3 (the only task modifying an API), Documentation Updates in task 3, Reuse Candidates in all tasks. This matches the task-description-template.md specification which says 'Omit sections that don't apply'.

assertion_results.text: Tasks document assumptions where they fill in missing details, labeled as assumptions pending clarification
assertion_results.evidence: All four tasks contain assumption notes labeled 'Ambiguity note (assumption pending clarification)': Task 1 — 'The feature description does not specify which entity fields should be searchable or how they should be weighted. We assume: SBOM name/description, Advisory title/description/severity, and Package name/license...'. Task 2 — two such notes about relevance criteria and performance targets. Task 3 — assumption about filter fields ('entity_type', 'severity', 'date_from'/'date_to'). Task 4 — assumption about backward compatibility meaning. The impact map also labels its assumptions as 'Assumption pending clarification'.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All four tasks contain '## Repository\ntrustify-backend' — a single repository name. Task 1 line 3: 'trustify-backend'. Task 2 line 3: 'trustify-backend'. Task 3 line 3: 'trustify-backend'. Task 4 line 3: 'trustify-backend'. None list multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All four tasks contain all four required sections: Task 1 — '## Target Branch' (line 5), '## Description' (line 8), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 36), '## Test Requirements' (line 44). Task 2 — '## Target Branch' (line 5), '## Description' (line 8), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 46), '## Test Requirements' (line 53). Task 3 — '## Target Branch' (line 5), '## Description' (line 8), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 55), '## Test Requirements' (line 65). Task 4 — '## Target Branch' (line 5), '## Description' (line 8), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 40), '## Test Requirements' (line 50).

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Checking all file paths against repo-backend.md: Task 1 Files to Modify: 'migration/src/lib.rs' (exists in manifest line 17). Files to Create: 'migration/src/m0002_search_fulltext/mod.rs' — follows the pattern of 'migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs' (manifest line 19) with a new migration name, which is a legitimate new file. Task 2 Files to Modify: 'modules/search/src/service/mod.rs' (manifest line 91), 'modules/search/src/lib.rs' (manifest line 89). Task 3 Files to Modify: 'modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs' (manifest line 93), 'modules/search/src/service/mod.rs' (manifest line 91), 'common/src/db/query.rs' (manifest line 27). Task 4 Files to Modify: 'tests/api/search.rs' (manifest line 113). All paths exist in the manifest or follow established naming patterns for new files.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 references: 'migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs' for module structure pattern. Task 2 references: 'modules/search/src/service/mod.rs' (SearchService location), 'common/src/model/paginated.rs' (PaginatedResults<T>), 'common/src/db/query.rs' (query builder helpers), 'common/src/error.rs' (AppError). Task 3 references: 'modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs', 'modules/search/src/service/mod.rs', 'common/src/db/query.rs', 'common/src/error.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs' (AdvisorySummary severity field), 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/list.rs' (query parameter extraction pattern). Task 4 references: 'tests/api/search.rs', 'tests/api/sbom.rs', 'tests/api/advisory.rs', and the 'assert_eq!(resp.status(), StatusCode::OK)' pattern. All Implementation Notes sections reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence:
Task 1 line 5-6: '## Target Branch
main'. Task 2 line 5-6: '## Target Branch
main'. Task 3 line 5-6: '## Target Branch
main'. Task 4 line 5-6: '## Target Branch
main'. All four tasks have Target Branch set to 'main'.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: All five output files contain digest markers with the correct format: impact-map.md: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:af43f96c967629f05abc142b200d0cabd1520e3c5d708de1ee924a2c1caf81b7' (64 hex chars). Task 1: 'sha256-md:9243a71c2b3214cc0098dc5bd64a7cb81a32b4a85d20952977128d10f9a03ca8' (64 hex chars). Task 2: 'sha256-md:84efee3f4940b70919269547496b9599219e4d86438fcc785574c584e975d8fa' (64 hex chars). Task 3: 'sha256-md:ea7c9136d4d5da3e892079b1bdfe5f9e64eea781ace6b1f6b44a93705cb9fda6' (64 hex chars). Task 4: 'sha256-md:9e6140ae4f6e2f314f0ed968a047ef37b957dce15711b8d8731517afc5d06ef9' (64 hex chars). All use the 'sha256-md:' prefix with exactly 64 lowercase hex characters. None are placeholders or abbreviated values.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All tasks include convention applicability sections with the prescribed rationale format. Task 1: 'Per CONVENTIONS.md §Module pattern: follow model/ + service/ + endpoints/ structure... Applies: task creates migration/src/m0002_search_fulltext/mod.rs matching the convention's migration file scope.' Task 2 has three conventions all with 'Applies: task modifies modules/search/src/service/mod.rs matching the convention's ...' format. Task 3 has four conventions each with specific file references in the prescribed format. Task 4: 'Applies: task modifies tests/api/search.rs matching the convention's test file scope.' No inapplicable conventions are listed with 'Not applicable' annotations. All rationales follow the prescribed format 'Applies: task modifies/creates <file> matching the convention's <scope signal>' rather than free-form prose.
